Gmail API Setup Guide

To use Gmail with Agent Mahoo, you need to create OAuth2 credentials in the Google Cloud Console.

1. Create Google Cloud Project

  1. Go to the Google Cloud Console.
  2. Create a new project (e.g., "Agent Mahoo Gmail").
  3. Navigate to APIs & Services > Library.
  4. Search for "Gmail API" and click Enable.

2. Configure OAuth Consent Screen

  1. Go to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen.
  2. Choose External User Type (or Internal if you are on Google Workspace).
  3. Fill in the required App information (name, email).
  4. In Scopes, add https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify and https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send.
  5. In Test users, add the Gmail address you want to use.

3. Create Credentials

  1. Go to APIs & Services > Credentials.
  2. Click Create Credentials > OAuth client ID.
  3. Select Web application as the Application type.
  4. Name it (e.g., "Agent Mahoo Web").
  5. Under Authorized redirect URIs, add:
    http://localhost:5000/gmail_oauth_callback
    Note: If you access Agent Mahoo via a different host, use that hostname/port instead.
  6. Click Create.
  7. Download the credentials.json file or copy its contents.

4. Link Account

  1. Go to Manage Accounts in Agent Mahoo settings.
  2. Click Add Account.
  3. Upload or paste the contents of your credentials.json.
  4. Click Authenticate via Google and complete the flow in the popup window.
